title: FROM fails to resolve local manifest with correct platform
body:
### Issue Description

`FROM` fails to reference a local manifest with the correct platform.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Start with the following two files:

**`Dockerfile.base`**:

```dockerfile
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM docker.io/alpine AS base
ARG TARGETPLATFORM
RUN echo $TARGETPLATFORM >>/targetplatform
```

**`Dockerfile.final`**:

```dockerfile
FROM foobase AS final
```

Then execute the following commands:

```sh
podman build --platform linux/arm64 --manifest foobase -f Dockerfile.base
podman build --platform linux/arm64 --manifest foofinal -f Dockerfile.final
```

### Describe the results you received

The second command fails with:

```
STEP 1/1: FROM foobase AS final
Resolving "foobase" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/10-unqualified-search-registries.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/foobase:latest...
Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://foobase:latest: reading manifest latest in docker.io/library/foobase: requested access to the resource is denied
```

The manifest sounds correct:

```
> podman manifest inspect foobase
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
    "manifests": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
            "size": 1181,
            "digest": "sha256:691729362b6b9f94cf33982b29a4de0af672213d92e2a122767afeaeeb8629d7",
            "platform": {
                "architecture": "arm64",
                "os": "linux"
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

Changing the second Dockerfile to use `FROM --platform=linux/arm64 foobase` results in the same error.

### Describe the results you expected

The second Dockerfile should be able to reference the image in the manifest with the correct platform.

comments.body: I think this is fixed in upstream. I am unable to reproduce this from upstream buildah.

Curious: it appears that something is wrong when committing the first image. With buildah 1.39.4:

- Using buildah for both images works
- Using podman for the first image and buildah for the second one doesn't work
- Using buildah for the first image and podman for the second one works

(Note, I need to `podman manifest rm foobase` between attempts, because images are appended to the manifest instead of replacing previous entries with the same platform.)

Ah, it seems like I can still reproduce with `buildah build --layers`. This flag is disabled by default with buildah, but enabled by default with podman.

Additionally, the bug cannot be reproduced with `podman build --layers=false`.
Could be that, I checked with `buildah` and by default `--layers` is `false` there.

Using `--platform` for a build quietly alters the pull policy to "newer" to try to make sure that we use a base image that matches the desired platform, and that would explain the `Trying to pull docker.io/library/foobase:latest...` message.

If that pull policy means we look at a registry and, not finding an image with the searched-for name there, fail, I think that's a bug.  Not noticing that we have a "locahost/foobase" locally and only prompting to expand "foobase" using the names of the configured registries before trying to pull the image is also a problem of some kind, but then I'm not sure I completely understand the details of the whole shortnames implementation.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I have also hit this issue. My initial situation was very similar to the OP, but after a bit of experimentation I managed to get it to fail sooner in the build using the `--all-platforms` flag for the final build.

```
podman build --platform linux/arm64 --manifest foobase -f Dockerfile.base
podman build --all-platforms --manifest foofinal -f Dockerfile.final
```

Now the second command fails almost immediately with

```
Error: base image name "localhost/foobase" didn't resolve to a manifest list
```

I believe this to be the same underlying error but without getting to the pull part of the process. I've also been testing the second command with `--pull=never` just to avoid the network trip for images I know aren't online.

I will start experimenting with `--layers false` to see how that affects my build.

**edit**

I forgot to mention that I'm using Podman 5.5.0 on Mac (arm64) and using `--platforms linux/arm64,linux/amd64` in the real build. Using `--platforms` instead of `--all-platforms` for the second build results in podman resolving the `arm64` image successfully but fails with errors like the OP for the `amd64` platform build job.

**edit2**

I tried all combinations of `--layers=false` on the base and final builds but still hit the issue each time.
